title: The Challenge of Homelessness
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Despite the wealth of many modern nations, (0) homelessness remains a persistent and deeply troubling problem. People become homeless for a wide variety of (17) reasons, including (18) unemployment, relationship breakdown, and mental health difficulties. The consequences of sleeping rough are (19) devastating, affecting both physical and mental health, and making it (20) extremely difficult to escape the cycle.||Charities and (21) voluntary organisations provide vital support, but long-term solutions require greater (22) government involvement. Building more (23) affordable housing and improving access to mental health services are two key priorities. Tackling homelessness requires a (24) coordinated effort from housing authorities, health services, and employers working closely together.

example.text: Despite the wealth of many modern nations, (0) HOMELESSNESS remains a persistent and deeply troubling problem. (HOME)

questions.explanation: The plural noun 'reasons' is formed from 'reason' by adding '-s'.
The noun 'unemployment' is formed from 'employ' by adding the prefix 'un-' and suffix '-ment'.
The adjective 'devastating' is formed from 'devastate' by adding the suffix '-ing'.
The adverb 'extremely' is formed from 'extreme' by replacing '-e' with '-ely'.
The adjective 'voluntary' is formed from 'volunteer' by replacing '-teer' with '-tary'.
The noun 'government' is formed from 'govern' by adding the suffix '-ment'.
The adjective 'affordable' is formed from 'afford' by adding the suffix '-able'.
The adjective 'coordinated' is formed from 'coordinate' by adding the suffix '-d'.
